steps.title: Identify the key claim needing support
steps.content: The passage establishes that peaking plants “can generate more megawatt-hours of power per year than some run-of-river plants.” The example must directly show this: a peaking plant producing more annual power than at least one run-of-river plant.

steps.title: Match the claim to the table
steps.content: Great Falls (peaking, 124,392 MWh/yr) generates far more power annually than either run-of-river plant in the table (Scanlon: 7,511; Kansas River: 15,345), directly proving the claim. The other three either lack table support (B), compare irrelevant plants (D), or contradict the claim (C). That matches choice A — A is correct.
